name: The I.W.C
whyWereHere:
The I.W.C. believes OWL has a problem, and unfortunately for everyone else, they think they are the solution. Led by loudmouth critic Will Komplane and enforced by heavy-handed muscle Buzz Kihl, the team operates like a walking comment section with authority issues. They claim they are here to “clean up” wrestling, correct bad behavior, and hold the roster to a higher standard—but really, they just want control.

Komplane does the talking, judging, and moral grandstanding, while Buzz makes sure nobody gets too comfortable disagreeing. Together, they turn every match into a lecture and every loss into somebody else’s fault. The I.W.C. came to OWL to censor chaos, punish imperfection, and prove that their version of wrestling is the only one that matters.

name: True North Elite
whyWereHere:
True North Elite was born inside Syrup City Wrestling Academy, where two very different kinds of privilege collided. Levi Martin came from money and wanted something that could finally challenge him. Connor Canada came from hockey, where his future as an all-world goalie was ruined by a temper he could never keep in the crease.

Together, they became a perfect problem: Levi brought polish, arrogance, and cold ambition, while Connor brought violence, anger, and the kind of mean streak that made opponents regret signing contracts. After running through the Canadian indies and collecting gold, True North Elite arrived in OWL looking for a bigger stage and better prey. They do not see themselves as hungry newcomers. They see themselves as superior athletes from the north, here to take what they believe already belongs to them.

name: B.T.T.I.T.W (Best Tag Team In The World)
whyWereHere:
BTTITW stands for Best Tag Team in the World, and yes, they picked the name before winning anything because humility was never part of the plan. Johnny Gargano and Zack Sabre Jr. formed out of tension, not friendship. They were two obsessive wrestling minds who kept clashing, countering, and testing each other until competition slowly turned into respect.

Their styles should not work together this well, but that is what makes them dangerous. Gargano brings speed, heart, counters, and big-match survival. Zack brings submissions, joint manipulation, arrogance, and the ability to make every opponent look like they skipped class. One fights with emotion, the other with calculation, and somehow they meet in the middle as a nightmare tag team.

BTTITW is not here to be liked. They are here to prove the name is not a joke.

name: BlackTide
whyWereHere: When Jeff Cobb and Brody King joined forces, OWL got hit by a natural disaster. Black Tide is power, violence, and destruction rolled into one team — two wrecking machines who don’t care about flash, approval, or playing nice. Cobb brings the explosive throws and impossible strength, while Brody brings the chaos and brutality that turns every fight into a nightmare. Together, they don’t just beat teams — they swallow them whole. Black Tide is here to wash over the division, drag the competition under, and leave nothing standing when the wave pulls back. If they’ve set their sights on the tag titles, it may already be too late.

name: Bounty Hunnaz
whyWereHere:
Bounty Hunnaz are not here to be fan favorites, role models, or tag-team purists. Gabe Kidd and Jacob Fatu formed around one simple idea: every roster has people who need to be dealt with, and they are more than willing to collect the price.

Kidd is the wild dog of the team—mouthy, reckless, and always ready to turn a match into a fight. Fatu is the monster behind him, a Samoan wrecking machine who makes sure nobody walks away once the hunt begins. Together, they feel less like a tag team and more like a warning notice. They do not chase glory the traditional way. They hunt targets, cash in damage, and leave teams wondering who put their name on the list.

name: Los Ingobernables: Plata o Plomo
whyWereHere:
Los Ingobernables: Plata o Plomo is not a normal tag team. It is an ultimatum. RUSH and Andrade operate with the same message for every team in OWL: accept their superiority, or get forced to understand it the hard way.

RUSH brings raw violence, pride, and the ability to turn any match into a fight no one is comfortable surviving. Andrade brings precision, arrogance, and the kind of ring IQ that makes every opening feel like a trap. Together, they are money and menace, luxury and brutality, style and punishment. They do not see the tag division as competition. They see it as territory.

The name says everything. Take the silver, take the beating, or get out of their way.

name: Double Dragon
whyWereHere:
Double Dragon is what happens when family, lucha libre, and competitive pride collide. Dragon Lee and Dralístico do not enter OWL as two random stars thrown together for convenience. They enter as brothers with shared history, shared blood, and very different ways of proving greatness.

Dragon Lee brings speed, precision, and controlled fire. Dralístico brings style, danger, and unpredictability. Together, they create a team that can outpace, outfly, and outthink almost anyone in the division. But underneath the masks, the moves, and the spectacle is something more personal: they are fighting to show that their family’s legacy is not finished being written.

Double Dragon is not here to simply compete. They are here to make the tag division feel the heat.

name: Hard Luck
whyWereHere:
Hard Luck is what happens when Irish pride meets bad intentions. Sheamus and Jordan Devlin did not come together because they needed friends. They came together because OWL’s tag division needed a warning. One is a battle-tested bruiser who has made a career out of turning matches into fights. The other is a bitter, skilled technician with something to prove and no patience for disrespect.

Together, they are rough, mean, and hard to game-plan for. Sheamus brings the power, pressure, and veteran punishment. Devlin brings the speed, counters, and nasty precision. If you try to outfight them, Sheamus will flatten you. If you try to outwrestle them, Devlin will twist you up.

The name says it all: when you draw Sheamus and Devlin, that is Hard Luck.

name: Dude...Bro
whyWereHere:
Dude…Bro sounds like a joke until the bell rings. Rob Van Dam and Matt Riddle might be one of OWL’s most unlikely pairings, but the chemistry is obvious: two laid-back fighters with explosive offense, strange timing, and enough confidence to make chaos look casual.

RVD brings the legendary cool factor, aerial creativity, and educated feet. Riddle brings MMA toughness, barefoot violence, and a reckless kind of joy for combat. Together, they do not move like a traditional tag team. They drift, strike, counter, improvise, and somehow land exactly where they need to be.

Opponents may laugh at the name, the attitude, or the vibe. Then the kicks start flying, the suplexes start landing, and suddenly everyone understands: Dude…Bro is relaxed, not weak.

name: Idolos De Oro
whyWereHere:
Ídolos de Oro is the kind of team that makes the tag division feel more expensive the second they arrive. Místico and Máscara Dorada bring elegance, speed, tradition, and spectacle wrapped in gold. They are not just two luchadores sharing a corner — they are two generations of lucha brilliance moving with the same purpose.

Místico is the icon: calm, precise, legendary, and impossible to rattle. Máscara Dorada is the golden spark: fearless, explosive, and hungry to prove he belongs beside the best. Together, they can overwhelm teams with speed, confuse them with rhythm, and embarrass them with creativity.

OWL’s tag division has power teams, violent teams, and loud teams. Ídolos de Oro brings something different: magic, legacy, and championship shine.

name: The Forgotten Family
whyWereHere:
The Forgotten Family is built on one simple truth: Samoan wrestling history is bigger than the names everyone already knows. Samoa Joe and Toa Liona are not tied to the famous Anoaʻi dynasty, and that is exactly the point. They do not come to OWL carrying someone else’s legacy. They come carrying their own anger, pride, and destruction.

Joe is the cold, calculating killer — a veteran who can break opponents mentally before finishing them physically. Toa is the wrecking ball — pure island power with no interest in slowing down. Together, they are not trying to be accepted into anyone’s bloodline. They are here to carve out their own.

The Forgotten Family is not a tribute. It is a warning: remember them now, or get remembered for what they did to you.

name: What Da Dog Doin'
whyWereHere:
What Da Dog Doin’ sounds unserious until the two dogs get loose. Bron Breakker and Clark Connors are not a polished tag team built on elegance or strategy. They are a pair of violent, hard-charging maniacs who treat every match like someone left the gate open.

Bron is the bulldozer: explosive, loud, powerful, and impossible to ignore once he starts moving. Connors is the bite: scrappy, mean, relentless, and more than happy to make a match ugly. Together, they bring dog-fight energy to OWL’s tag division — barking, charging, throwing bodies, and daring anyone to stand their ground.

The name may get a laugh at first. Then the bell rings, and the joke becomes a problem.

name: Fraxiom
whyWereHere:
Fraxiom is controlled chaos with championship proof behind it. Nathan Frazer and Axiom should not work as smoothly as they do. Frazer is reckless speed, all instinct and acceleration. Axiom is masked precision, all timing and calculation. But together, they turn contrast into chemistry.

They came together after proving they could push each other as opponents, then discovered they were even harder to handle as partners. Their NXT run made them former champions, with WWE listing their reign from September 2024 to April 2025, and both later debuted on SmackDown in April 2025.

In OWL, Fraxiom brings a different kind of threat to the tag division. Power teams can hit harder. Brawlers can fight dirtier. But nobody makes the match move faster. One second of hesitation, and Fraxiom is already three counters ahead.

name: Southern Cross
whyWereHere:
Southern Cross is Australian violence with no apology attached. Mark Davis and Bronson Reed are not a flashy pretty-boy tag team, and they are damn sure not here to dance around the division. They are two heavy hitters from down under who turned size, strength, and bad intentions into a team identity.

Davis brings the tag-team instincts, the grit, and the brawler’s timing. Reed brings the overwhelming mass, speed, and destruction that makes every impact feel final. Together, they are not just big — they are coordinated, mean, and built to make opponents feel smaller by the minute.

The name Southern Cross carries pride, but the team carries punishment. When Davis and Reed step across the ring, the message is simple: look up, because the sky is about to fall.

name: Deddley Sistaz
whyWereHere:
Martha May and Chevonne Deddley are the tag-team engine of The Deddley Sistaz: one part wrecking ball, one part controlled chaos, and entirely too comfortable around wooden furniture.

Martha May brings the power, the volume, and the kind of aggression that turns a normal match into a family emergency. Chevonne brings speed, timing, and enough awareness to aim that destruction in the right direction. Together, they fight with the natural chemistry of women who have been causing trouble side by side their entire lives.

They do not want clean victories or polite applause. They want noise, impact, and the sight of another team wondering how everything went wrong so quickly. When Martha and Chevonne arrive, the table is not part of the decoration. It is part of the strategy.

name: Her-t Business
whyWereHere:
The Her-t Business is not a tag team. It is a family investment with fists attached.

Tiffany Omari Porter brings structure, strategy, and cold executive control. Victoria Imani Porter brings flair, speed, and the kind of attitude that turns a normal match into personal humiliation. Together, T.O.P. and V.I.P. operate like two sides of their father’s empire: one handles the negotiation, the other handles the damage.

With MVP at ringside as their manager, the Porters do not enter OWL looking for opportunity. They enter like opportunity has already been purchased. Every match is treated like a business meeting where the opposing team failed to read the fine print.

The Her-t Business is simple: bring the value, bring the violence, and leave with the win.

name: Lovers Of Liberty
whyWereHere: The Lovers of Liberty are chaos in red, white, and bruised. Kim McCratt and Connie Servativv may stand on opposite sides of the aisle, argue every talking point, and disagree on just about everything—but they are perfectly united when it comes to beating people down. Their chemistry comes from conflict: one polished and tactical, the other stern and punishing, both too stubborn to back down from anyone. Together, they weaponize division and turn debate into destruction. Their team identity is built on political theater, verbal jabs, and calculated violence, making them one of the most entertaining and dangerous duos in the division. And once they hit The Swing State—a giant swing into a perfectly timed dropkick—the result is usually bipartisan pain.

name: Kill Fight Kill: The Owensteens
whyWereHere: The Owensteens are sisterhood weaponized. Keva brings the indie-era brutality: wild brawling, reckless punishment, and a love for turning matches into survival tests. Kayla brings the prizefighter polish: smarter pacing, sharper trash talk, and the ability to steal victory while everyone is watching Keva cause the explosion. Together, they are Kill Fight Kill, a team built on one simple philosophy: hurt them first, hurt them louder, and make sure they remember who did it. They argue, shove each other, and occasionally look one bad decision away from fighting themselves—but once the bell rings, their chemistry becomes terrifying. The Owensteens do not care about being role models. They care about winning, surviving, and leaving bodies behind.

name: The Harvey Girlz
whyWereHere: The Harvey Girlz are what happens when sisterhood meets pure Xtreme obsession. Raised in Cameron, North Carolina, Katt and Jess Harvey grew up idolizing high-risk, high-heart tag team wrestling and built their whole identity around living without fear. Katt brings the grit, leadership, and inventive edge, while Jess brings the reckless fire and thrill-seeking chaos. Together, they are a nonstop storm of dives, grit, and guts. They don’t just wrestle to win — they wrestle to steal the night and make every crowd feel something. Tables, ladders, risks, pain, passion — none of it scares them. The Harvey Girlz live for the rush, the bond, and the belief that the biggest rewards come to the ones willing to crash the hardest. Two sisters, one mission: Xtreme.

name: Southern Heat
whyWereHere: Coming out of Sweetcorn County, Mississippi, Southern Heat are loud, proud, dangerous, and impossible to ignore. They built their name working rough local shows, county-fair cards, biker bars, and late-night spots where the crowd wanted attitude as much as action. Between shifts at the Rusty Nail Gentlemen’s Club and nights in the ring, they learned how to control a room fast — whether with charm, trash talk, or a fist to the mouth. They fight with southern swagger, backroad grit, and just enough wild energy to make every match feel like it could turn into a bar fight. Stormy brings the power and presence, while Daisha brings the mouth, the mayhem, and the sneaky edge. Together, they are Southern Heat — all sass, smoke, and hard-hitting chaos.

name: Crimson Lotus
whyWereHere:
Crimson Lotus is one of the most unsettling teams in OWL because nobody really knows anything about them. They arrive in a black limo, step out in matching kitsune masks, walk straight to the ring, handle business, and disappear before anyone can stop them. No interviews. No social media. No backstage friendships. No wasted motion. The only time they ever seem to “speak” is when they’re in the middle of a match, exchanging short commands in Japanese as they dissect opponents with eerie precision.

They carry themselves less like wrestlers and more like collectors sent to retrieve something they believe already belongs to them. Every movement is deliberate, every strike looks practiced, and every victory feels less like celebration and more like punishment delivered on schedule. To Crimson Lotus, the ring is not a stage — it is a hunting ground.

Together, they feel less like a normal tag team and more like a sealed pact. They barely need words, only glances and instinct. One breaks opponents down, the other capitalizes. One creates fear, the other controls it. Their chemistry is so exact that it feels unnatural, which only adds to the mystique.

Crimson Lotus doesn’t chase approval, and they don’t care about being understood. They are here for domination, and they intend to make the women’s tag division feel watched, hunted, and eventually conquered.

name: Bad Intentions
whyWereHere:
Bad Intentions is exactly what the name says: two women who know how to smile for the camera while planning something ugly behind it. Liv Morgan brings the chaos, mind games, and emotional volatility. Anna Jay brings the colder confidence, ring control, and mean streak. Together, they feel like trouble dressed as star power — the kind of team that can turn a normal tag match into betrayal, humiliation, and violence with one glance across the apron.

They are not built like a clean babyface friendship or a traditional tag unit. This is chemistry through shared attitude. Liv creates the distraction. Anna lands the punishment. Liv makes it messy. Anna makes it final. Bad Intentions does not need everyone to trust them

name: Dont Fuck With Texas
whyWereHere: Don’t F*ck With Texas is less of a catchy team name and more of a warning. Hailing from Dallas, Big Tex Colt Stetson and Buck Austin are former ranch-hands who built their lives on hard work, grit, and the kind of toughness you don’t learn in a gym. They don’t care about flash, politics, or who’s trending—they care about respect, pride, and dropping anybody who thinks Texas is just cowboy cosplay. Colt brings the size, power, and intimidating presence of a foreman ready to settle business, while Buck adds raw strength with the agility of a man who spent years taming animals meaner than most wrestlers. Together, they see OWL as a fight for territory, and anybody standing in their way is getting Taken ’Em Out Back.

name: House Money
whyWereHere: House Money is what happens when two arrogant high rollers decide the rules only apply to everyone else. Mikey “Makin’” Millions and Chassion Cash walk into OWL dressed like the afterparty, talking like kings, and treating the tag division like their personal casino floor. To them, every opponent is just another sucker sitting down at the table thinking they’ve got a chance. Mikey brings the loud charisma and headline energy, while Cash brings the cool, calculated edge that makes the team even more dangerous. Together they fight with the confidence of men who believe they can never truly lose, because in their minds they’re never risking anything—they’re playing with House Money. And once they hit House Always Wins, the night is over and the debt is collected.

name: The Funky Homosapiens
whyWereHere: Straight out of Harlem, The Funky Homosapiens are what happens when Parliament-Funkadelic, disco nightlife, and professional wrestling collide at full volume. Dynamite Disco Dave and Boogie Down Barry arrive dressed like the party already started three hours ago—and somehow they are still the most dangerous men in the room. Their entrances are loud, their gear is brighter than arena lighting, and there may occasionally be a suspicious dusting of “dance-floor enthusiasm” nearby, but dismissing them as a joke is a fast way to get knocked out. Dave supplies explosive speed and chaotic energy, while Barry brings old-school power, rhythm, and hips with their own gravitational pull. Once Groove Theory connects, the party stops for exactly three seconds.

name: Genn - Eric Varsity Club
whyWereHere:
The Genn-Eric Varsity Club believes professional wrestling has forgotten its roots. Allen Genn and Eric Shields aren't interested in catchphrases, viral moments, or winning over crowds—they're here to restore respect for technical wrestling by humiliating anyone who can't survive on the mat.

Former collegiate stars from Ann Arbor, the pair approaches every match like a championship wrestling tournament. They isolate limbs, grind opponents into exhaustion, and exploit every opening with ruthless efficiency. While fans cheer for spectacular offense, the Varsity Club takes pride in making every contest slow, uncomfortable, and unavoidable.

They don't call themselves the future of wrestling—they call themselves the standard. And when Great Lakes Landing finally drops an opponent, it's simply the final lesson in why fundamentals always beat flash.
